commit.message:
am2pla-site: emit bullets between category pills (#2388) Per maintainer feedback (and andy's review of the live preview), the grid layout between the page heading and the apps table still felt loose — pills were single-line and short, but the empty horizontal space inside each grid cell padded them apart visually. Pair with a website-side CSS change that switches the container from a CSS Grid (with uniform cells) to a flex layout (pills pack tight to their content width, gap controls spacing). Bullets emitted as .cat-sep spans between pills give a clear visual separator at that tighter spacing. The HTML emitted for a category page now looks like:
AppImages • am-utils • ...
aria-hidden so screen readers don't announce the bullet between every link in the nav. Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

commit.message:
am2pla-site: emit categories list as a CSS grid of pill-links (#2384) _categories_buttons() previously emitted a single line of bold-italic markdown links separated by " - ". On a wide page that's a long wrapping run; on a narrow viewport it's worse. Switch to raw HTML:
AppImages am-utils ...
CSS lives in Portable-Linux-Apps.github.io's assets/css/style.scss (.cat-grid uses grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, ...) for responsive reflow; .cat-pill--all gives AppImages an accent background and bold weight so it stands out). Light/dark variants mirror the existing theme switch in that file. Order is alphabetical (AppImages first as the meta-category, then the CATEGORIES list which is already alphabetical). Applies in every place that calls _categories_buttons: index.md, apps.md, appimages.md, and each category page. Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

Light/dark variants mirror the existing theme switch in that file. Order is alphabetical (AppImages first as the meta-category, then the CATEGORIES list which is already alphabetical). Applies in every place that calls _categories_buttons: index.md, apps.md, appimages.md, and each category page. Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)

commit.message:
Fixed _check_version_filters() in APP-MANAGER to be more accurate & added new test-am-versions.sh to validate it (#2385)

* Added an extra check for test-am-utils.sh to catch the redownload bug.

* Regression testing updates:
- Added new test-am-versions to prevent regressions in _check_version_filters()
- Added test-am-version to regression test list.

* Fixed _check_version_filters() so that test-am-versions will pass.

* Fixes to test-am-versions.sh

* Added goawk and print awk version before test start.

* Fixes and updates:
- Updated _check_version_if_version_file_exists() to use _check_version_filters() without any modifiers.
- Added more complex test strings to test-am-versions.sh.

* Updated version checks to print longer version numbers if it matches.

* Updated test-am-version to test more complex strings.

* Test updates: Suppress error when checkng awk version (fails with original/busybox awk).

* Simplified test-am-versions.sh awk binary iteration.

* Added nawk and busybox awk testing into test-am-versions.sh

* Bumped AM version

commit.message:
am2pla-site: place search bar on every list page, fix apps.json count (#2383)

Three related bugs in tools/am2pla-site:

1. ADD_SEARCH_BAR redirected output to a hard-coded "./apps" file
   instead of the page being built, so the search bar ended up in an
   orphaned untracked file and never appeared on apps.html.
   Parameterise it to write to "$1" and pass the target file from
   each caller.

2. _category_list_header and _appimages_list_header never invoked
   ADD_SEARCH_BAR, so the 24 category pages and appimages.md had no
   search bar at all. Mirror what _applications_list_header does and
   call ADD_SEARCH_BAR right after the existing search-tool hint
   text.

3. _update_json iterated over $ARGS (all entries, including the
   "items" — ffwa-* webapps and the kdegames/kdeutils/node/
   platform-tools metapackages), so apps.json contained items and
   the home page's "X apps in the database" status line was
   inflated. The script already excludes the same patterns when
   computing APPS_NUMBER on line 24; apply the same exclusion in
   _update_json so the JSON contains apps only.

Verified locally by running the patched script against a fresh
worktree of Portable-Linux-Apps.github.io main: search bar appears on
apps.md, appimages.md, and every category page; apps.json no longer
contains the excluded items.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree 3f83719edadf361bc6b7d80dcf18adaf6ecad6a3
parent 0226034b5edba5e16e48ac46025517b803f30cc7
author Andy Alt <andy5995@users.noreply.github.com> 1779498513 -0500
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1779498513 +0200

am2pla-site: place search bar on every list page, fix apps.json count (#2383)

Three related bugs in tools/am2pla-site:

1. ADD_SEARCH_BAR redirected output to a hard-coded "./apps" file
   instead of the page being built, so the search bar ended up in an
   orphaned untracked file and never appeared on apps.html.
   Parameterise it to write to "$1" and pass the target file from
   each caller.

2. _category_list_header and _appimages_list_header never invoked
   ADD_SEARCH_BAR, so the 24 category pages and appimages.md had no
   search bar at all. Mirror what _applications_list_header does and
   call ADD_SEARCH_BAR right after the existing search-tool hint
   text.

3. _update_json iterated over $ARGS (all entries, including the
   "items" — ffwa-* webapps and the kdegames/kdeutils/node/
   platform-tools metapackages), so apps.json contained items and
   the home page's "X apps in the database" status line was
   inflated. The script already excludes the same patterns when
   computing APPS_NUMBER on line 24; apply the same exclusion in
   _update_json so the JSON contains apps only.

Verified locally by running the patched script against a fresh
worktree of Portable-Linux-Apps.github.io main: search bar appears on
apps.md, appimages.md, and every category page; apps.json no longer
contains the excluded items.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
